intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Fixed left sidebar with experiment list reduces cognitive load for quick navigation vs. collapsible design
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and quick access to experiment list; likely prefers stable reference frame over screen real estate
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes maximizing content space or prefers mobile-first mobile-friendly; may want navigation hidden until needed

evidence.hypothesis: Fixed left sidebar with experiment list reduces cognitive load for quick navigation vs. collapsible design
evidence.implication: User values persistent context and quick access to experiment list; likely prefers stable reference frame over screen real estate

facade.hypothesis: A friendly, conversational tone (vs. clinical/formal) will make users more likely to engage consistently with health tracking
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality: friendly vs. professional
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers warm, approachable language—'How's it going?' over 'Enter vitals.' Dashboard should feel like chatting with a friend, not visiting a doctor's office.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and authority—clinical precision, structured forms, professional distance. Dashboard should feel trustworthy through formality, not familiarity.

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboard users prefer gentle, approachable UI over clinical sharpness
facade.axisTargeted: Softness vs. sharpness in component design
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Design system should use 12-16px border radius, curved buttons, pill-shaped badges; soften the clinical feel of data displays
facade.rejectImplies: Explore sharp angles, minimal border radius (0-4px), geometric shapes; reinforce precision and clinical credibility

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking health experiments benefit from calm, organic layouts that feel restorative rather than clinical or data-dense
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool, calm vs energetic
facade.label: Soft, breathing space
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward layouts with generous padding, curved elements, muted palettes, and a sense of ease—suggests preference for meditative UX over efficiency-first dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers compact, purposeful layouts with clear boundaries and efficient data presentation—suggests lean toward calm-but-structured or energetic-and-crisp over soft-and-airy

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated transitions between dashboard views over instant state changes, as it maintains context and feels less jarring when switching between different health metrics or time periods.
facade.axisTargeted: Animated transitions vs instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth slide in
facade.acceptImplies: Design system should prioritize fluid motion for view changes, tab switches, and metric updates. Consider easing curves and duration that feel natural but not sluggish.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions. Explore zero-latency transitions, instant loads, or toggle-style state changes without animation overlays.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory feedback on progress vs. quiet, non-judgmental tracking space
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
Whether user wants instant feedback on logged data vs. delayed analysis and weekly digests

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory feedback on progress vs. quiet, non-judgmental tracking space
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on logged data vs. delayed analysis and weekly digests
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users want to see their key health metric (weight, steps, sleep hours) prominently displayed at a glance, rather than requiring a chart or narrative explanation to understand their current status
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — whether the dashboard leads with a single dominant number or distributes information across visualizations
facade.label: Big number, small context
facade.acceptImplies: User values at-a-glance metric spotting; dashboard should feature large typography for primary measurements (weight, steps, glucose), minimal supporting text
facade.rejectImplies: User wants context layers (trends, comparisons, goals) before engaging with any single metric; prefer narrative or chart-first layouts that frame the number within broader patterns

facade.hypothesis: Dark background with light text reduces eye strain during evening health tracking and creates focused, clinical feel vs. bright interface that feels more energetic and approachable
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: User prioritizes comfort and clinical precision over warmth; likely works with dashboard in low-light settings; may prefer data-dense, high-contrast layouts
facade.rejectImplies: User wants bright, optimistic visual language for health tracking; prefers warm, encouraging aesthetic; may value approachability over intensity
